title: Authenticated Encryption with AES-GCM · Go Semantics Case: Crypto Go Gcm Nonce System
summary: A multi-tenant service reuses GCM nonces after restart, omits tenant and version context from additional data, and releases plaintext before authentication succeeds. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gcm nonce system increment in versioned AES-GCM record envelope. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Design a crash-, concurrency-, rollback-, and replication-safe GCM nonce strategy within the construction limits.
Use AEAD Seal and Open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
package main

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	key := make([]byte, 16)
	key[0] = 3
	block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
	if err != nil { panic(err) }
	aead, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
	if err != nil { panic(err) }
	nonce := make([]byte, aead.NonceSize())
	nonce[len(nonce)-1] = 3
	aad := []byte("v1|tenant-a|record-3")
	ciphertext := aead.Seal(nil, nonce, []byte("private-evidence"), aad)
	plaintext, openErr := aead.Open(nil, nonce, ciphertext, aad)
	tampered := append([]byte(nil), ciphertext...)
	tampered[len(tampered)-1] ^= 1
	_, tamperErr := aead.Open(nil, nonce, tampered, aad)
	fmt.Printf("opened=%t plaintext=%s tamper_rejected=%t\n", openErr == nil, plaintext, tamperErr != nil)
}
// Worked focus: crypto-go-gcm-nonce-system — Design a crash-, concurrency-, rollback-, and replication-safe GCM nonce strategy within the construction limits.

Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design a crash-, concurrency-, rollback-, and replication-safe GCM nonce strategy within the construction limits.

For Cryptography and Go Defend the Crypto Go Gcm Nonce System model · Theory Crypto Go Gcm Nonce System, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Random GCM nonces can be generated forever without collision analysis.
